findings.claim: The new enterprise/poa and enterprise/group modules (a new proof-of-authority module and a group module split into an enterprise directory) are introduced as new Go modules with dependencies on the core SDK, cometbft, and store/v2, without an accompanying decision record explaining why these were split out as standalone enterprise modules with their own module boundaries.
findings.consequence: A maintainer six months from now would not know why the poa and group functionality were carved out into separate 'enterprise' modules with independent versioning and replace directives, rather than remaining part of the core SDK or an existing module.

findings.claim: The store/v2 module adds a dependency on github.com/hashicorp/go-plugin, a library for building plugin-based process architectures, which is an architectural choice (out-of-process plugin boundary) that a later reader would need reasoning for.
findings.consequence: Without a recorded decision, future maintainers cannot tell why the storage layer depends on a plugin RPC framework, whether it defines a plugin boundary for storage backends, or whether it can be safely removed.
